title: Deferred Operation v1
description: Initial 202 Accepted control payload for an explicitly opted-in deferred operation. This is not domain data; it tells the caller that processing is suspended and may be resumed by polling or a stored continuation.

schema.description: Schema tag for the initial deferred operation payload.

status.description: Initial control status. Callers MUST suspend downstream domain processing instead of treating this payload as ordinary output.

operation/id.description: Stable id for the deferred operation. Implementations SHOULD derive it deterministically from the operation kind and an idempotency seed when duplicate suppression matters.

operation/kind.description: Operation class that owns the continuation semantics, e.g. sensorium.directive.invoke or json-e-flow.step.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the host accepted the deferred operation.

retry_after_seconds.description: Host-clamped lower bound before the caller or scheduler should poll/resume the operation. It maps to HTTP Retry-After for HTTP surfaces.

expires_at.description: Host-clamped absolute deadline after which the operation MUST be treated as expired if no terminal result exists.

status_href.description: Optional local status endpoint for polling. Omitted when the caller must resume through a stored continuation rather than HTTP polling.

cancel_href.description: Optional local cancel endpoint. Absence means cancellation is unsupported for this operation kind.

cancel/unavailable-reason.description: Reason cancellation is not available for this operation. Exactly one of cancel_href or cancel/unavailable-reason MUST be present.

correlation/id.description: Optional correlation id from the parent flow or workflow.

causal/context.description: Optional canonical P081 causal context preserved across the deferred operation boundary.

audit/outcome-ref.description: Optional reference to the host/runtime audit outcome that recorded acceptance of this deferred operation.

continuation.description: Explicit serializable continuation context. It is a host/runtime value, not a captured language stack.

diagnostics.description: Optional diagnostics for operator and caller visibility.

extensions.description: Open extension map for experimental or deployment-local metadata. Stable protocol fields MUST be promoted to first-class schema properties.
